# PR #6 — Add standalone AppView validation experiment against live Jetstream

source: https://github.com/croftc/SecurityPolicy/pull/6

captured: 2026-06-15 (via cpettet_croftc). State at capture: OPEN. 19 files, +4998/-0.

branch: claude/appview-validation-jetstream-jpov76 → main

> Imported into CroftCommunity/experiments as code-forward proof-of-value. The
> companion AI coding transcript is in CODING-TRANSCRIPT.md; the experiment's own
> findings are in README.md.

---

## PR description

## What this is

A **self-contained** Rust experiment (`experiments/appview-validation/`) that builds the simplest *complete* AT Protocol AppView — **ingest → index → serve** — and validates it against the **real public atproto network** via Jetstream. Depends on no prior experiment.

The primary objective is **learning and validation, not feature breadth**. The headline deliverable is a field-by-field comparison of a *predicted* event shape against the real Jetstream wire shape, plus observed event rate and an honest friction log.

## Architecture (three components, clean ingest seam)

- **Ingest** (`record_source.rs`, `jetstream.rs`): a `RecordSource` trait yields normalized `RecordEvent`s; `JetstreamSource` is a real WebSocket consumer (`tokio-tungstenite`) of the live public Jetstream, server-side filtered by collection NSID. Events are parsed from **raw JSON on purpose** — a pre-baked Jetstream crate would mask the very modeling gap the experiment measures (documented deviation from the brief's crate suggestion).
- **Index** (`index.rs`): disposable SQLite projection keyed by AT-URI; create (upsert) / update (replace) / delete (remove).
- **Serve** (`server.rs`): axum XRPC-shaped read API with `getRecentPosts` and a `GROUP BY` hydration query `getLikeCountsBySubject`.
- **Reality check** (`report.rs`): diffs the predicted `RecordEvent` against the first real event.

## Connectivity gate (run before any feature code) — PASSED

DNS + TLS + `HTTP/1.1 101 Switching Protocols` on `wss://jetstream2.us-east.bsky.network/subscribe`, live events streamed; crates.io reachable. No stubbing.

## Headline findings (hypothesis vs reality)

| Prediction | Verdict | Reality |
|---|---|---|
| `cid` at event top level | **WRONG** | lives at `commit.cid` — likely silent-null bug for a real builder |
| every frame is `kind:"commit"` | **WRONG** | `identity`/`account` frames arrive even on a **collection-filtered** stream — `wantedCollections` does not filter event *kinds* |
| `time_us` cursor, units unknown | **HELD** | confirmed **microseconds** since epoch |
| delete has no record/cid | **HELD** | confirmed |
| `record.createdAt` ISO string | **HELD** | but precision varies (millis vs micros) |

Present-but-unmodeled: `commit.rev` (repo revision TID). **Observed rate:** `app.bsky.feed.like` ~185 ev/s vs `app.bsky.feed.post` ~40 ev/s — a single collection is already a torrent.

## Friction log (highlights)

- `rustls 0.23` panics without an explicit crypto provider → install `ring` at startup.
- `libsqlite3-sys 0.38` won't build on stable 1.94.1 (`cfg_select!`, E0658) → pinned `rusqlite 0.32.1`.

## Validated vs still unproven

**Validated:** real ingest → index → serve with genuine hydration, and the event shape/rate/behavior mapped against assumptions. **Unproven (named in README):** publishing/OAuth, custom lexicons on the network, PDS backfill, moderation/labels, scale.

Reproduce: `cd experiments/appview-validation && cargo run` (bounded, ~80s, terminates cleanly). Full report in the experiment README.

https://claude.ai/code/session_015dnuu8XfvbdAeLuFrYeVF6

<details>
<summary>📝 Walkthrough</summary>

## Walkthrough

A Rust experiment: normalize Jetstream commit frames to RecordEvent, project into a disposable SQLite index, serve two XRPC-like read endpoints, and provide multiple binaries (main, publish, bootstrap, feed, firehose, labeler, local) plus lexicon validation and shape-vs-hypothesis reporting.

## Changes

**AppView Validation Pipeline**

|Layer / File(s)|Summary|
|---|---|
|**Data model and parsing contract** <br> `experiments/appview-validation/src/record_source.rs`|Defines `Action`, `RecordEvent`, `ParseOutcome`, and `parse_frame` which normalizes commit frames, classifies NonCommit/Malformed, and resolves `cid` from `commit` or top-level.|
|**Jetstream WebSocket source** <br> `experiments/appview-validation/src/jetstream.rs`|`JetstreamSource` builds subscribe URL with `wantedCollections`, `wantedDids`, and `cursor`; `run` connects via WebSocket, decodes frames (text/binary UTF-8 handling), emits ParseOutcome to caller, counts commits, supports early-stop, and enforces time/max_commits bounds.|
|**AT Protocol helpers** <br> `experiments/appview-validation/src/atproto.rs`|Reqwest client/session creation, create/delete record helpers, DID doc resolution → PDS endpoint/handle, list/get record, get_profile, and `parse_at_uri`.|
|**SQLite index projection** <br> `experiments/appview-validation/src/index.rs`|`Index::open` recreates DB and schema; `Index::apply` deletes or upserts create/update JSON and derived columns; includes `apply_batch`, `add_label`, `recent_posts`, `top_liked_subjects`, `records_json`, cursor checkpointing, and counts.|
|**XRPC HTTP server and endpoints** <br> `experiments/appview-validation/src/server.rs`|Axum `router(db_path)` exposes `/xrpc/com.example.getRecentPosts` (hydrates `replyTo`) and `/xrpc/com.example.getLikeCountsBySubject` (grouped like counts), with `limit` clamping and a minimal `http_get` helper.|
|**Validation and shape reporting** <br> `experiments/appview-validation/src/report.rs`|`Findings` aggregator and `print_shape_report` compare consumed envelope paths vs actual, list unmodeled fields, and run numbered checks (cid placement, `time_us` plausibility, `createdAt`); `print_findings` prints ingest stats.|
|**Orchestration and execution** <br> `experiments/appview-validation/src/main.rs`|Main binary installs TLS provider, runs two ingests (posts, likes), captures first commit for shape report, updates index/stats, starts Axum server, issues live GETs to both endpoints, and prints findings and disposability note.|
|**Publish-loop validation** <br> `experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/publish.rs`|Publish CLI authenticates with env creds, creates test records, watches Jetstream replay for rkey observation using JetstreamSource, applies commits to Index, reports observation, and deletes records.|
|**Bootstrap lifecycle proofs** <br> `experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/bootstrap.rs`|Backfill→live-tail no-gap proof and cursor persistence/crash recovery proof implemented with Jetstream replay, saved cursors, and index upserts; includes teardown to delete published records.|
|**Trending feed & hydration** <br> `experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/feed.rs`|Time-bounded ingest of posts+likes, computes top liked subjects, hydrates subjects by resolving DID→PDS/handle and fetching post text for display snippets.|
|**Firehose capacity test** <br> `experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/firehose.rs`|Measure batched insert capacity; bounded producer/consumer with queue/backpressure tracking, batch writes via `apply_batch`, and post-run metrics/headroom calculation.|
|**Labeler subscription experiment** <br> `experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/labeler.rs`|Subscribe to labeler DID endpoint, decode two-part DAG-CBOR frames (header+body), extract labels, track protocol shapes and per-label fields, and report taxonomy.|
|**Local deterministic proof harness** <br> `experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/local.rs`|FixtureSource and helpers to run lexicon comprehension, backfill, moderation labels, and batch vs per-row indexing benchmarks locally without network.|
|**Custom lexicon** <br> `experiments/appview-validation/src/lexicon.rs`|Typed `ValidationNote` schema and `comprehend` to validate `$type` and deserialize with readable rejection reasons.|
|**Project configuration and documentation** <br> `.gitignore`, `Cargo.toml`, `README.md`|Ignore local build and SQLite artifacts; pinned Cargo dependencies and features; README documents architecture, run steps, measured outcomes, surprises, and scope.|
|**Library exports** <br> `experiments/appview-validation/src/lib.rs`|Re-exports modules (`atproto`, `index`, `jetstream`, `record_source`, `report`, `server`, `lexicon`).|

## Estimated code review effort

🎯 4 (Complex) | ⏱️ ~45 minutes

- Flagged actionable issues:
  - Binary-frame UTF-8 handling: binary frames are attempted as UTF-8 and treated as Malformed on failure. Fix: add a distinct Binary outcome or route binary frames to a binary parser rather than classifying as Malformed.
  - SQLite concurrency: using Arc<Mutex<rusqlite::Connection>> inside async handlers serializes DB access and may create contention. Fix: run DB ops via spawn_blocking or adopt a connection pool (r2d2/sqlx) for concurrent async use.
  - Index error visibility: Index apply errors are skipped in main; systemic failures can be hidden. Fix: surface/sketch aggregate skipped-fail counts and provide a fail-fast option for CI.
- Data/input parameters at risk: Jetstream subscribe query params (wantedCollections/wantedDids/cursor), pinned rusqlite features (bundled), TLS/rustls provider initialization, and environment credentials for publish/bootstrap binaries.

## Suggested reviewers

- croftc/infosec

<details>
<summary>🧹 Nitpick comments (2)</summary><blockquote>

<details>
<summary>experiments/appview-validation/src/server.rs (1)</summary><blockquote>

`60-69`: _⚡ Quick win_

**Multiple `unwrap()` calls will panic and crash the server on database errors.**

Lines 60, 61-69, and similar calls in `get_like_counts_by_subject` (lines 92-102) panic on lock poisoning or SQL errors instead of returning proper HTTP error responses. While acceptable for an experiment, consider using `?` and mapping errors to axum responses for graceful degradation.





<details>
<summary>♻️ Proposed fix using Result return types</summary>

```diff
 async fn get_recent_posts(
     State(st): State<AppState>,
     Query(p): Query<LimitParam>,
-) -> Json<Value> {
+) -> Result<Json<Value>, (axum::http::StatusCode, String)> {
     let limit = clamp_limit(p.limit);
-    let conn = st.db.lock().unwrap();
+    let conn = st.db.lock().map_err(|e| 
+        (axum::http::StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("Lock error: {e}")))?;
     let mut stmt = conn
         .prepare(
             "SELECT uri, did, text, created_at, reply_parent
              FROM records
              WHERE collection = 'app.bsky.feed.post'
              ORDER BY cursor DESC
              LIMIT ?1",
         )
-        .unwrap();
+        .map_err(|e| 
+            (axum::http::StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("SQL error: {e}")))?;
     let rows = stmt
         .query_map([limit], |row| {
             let reply_parent: Option<String> = row.get(4)?;
             Ok(json!({
                 "uri": row.get::<_, String>(0)?,
                 "author": row.get::<_, String>(1)?,
                 "text": row.get::<_, Option<String>>(2)?,
                 "createdAt": row.get::<_, Option<String>>(3)?,
                 "replyTo": reply_parent,
             }))
         })
-        .unwrap()
+        .map_err(|e| 
+            (axum::http::StatusCode::INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR, format!("Query error: {e}")))?
         .filter_map(Result::ok)
         .collect::<Vec<_>>();
-    Json(json!({ "posts": rows, "count": rows.len() }))
+    Ok(Json(json!({ "posts": rows, "count": rows.len() })))
 }
```

Apply similar changes to `get_like_counts_by_subject`.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/server.rs` around lines 60 - 69, The code
currently uses st.db.lock().unwrap() and .prepare(...).unwrap() (and similar
unwraps in get_like_counts_by_subject) which will panic on lock poisoning or SQL
errors; change the handlers to return Result<impl IntoResponse, impl
IntoResponse> (or a common error type implementing IntoResponse), replace
lock().unwrap() with st.db.lock().map_err(|e| map_db_error(e))? and replace
conn.prepare(...).unwrap() with conn.prepare(...).map_err(|e| map_sql_error(e))?
so errors propagate instead of panicking, and map those errors to proper axum
HTTP responses (e.g., 500 JSON body) in the error-to-response conversion; apply
the same pattern to get_like_counts_by_subject and any other locations that used
unwrap() on DB operations.
```

</details>

<!-- cr-comment:v1:221a9b0a2c18990218f1a65b -->

</blockquote></details>
<details>
<summary>experiments/appview-validation/src/main.rs (1)</summary><blockquote>

`165-169`: _💤 Low value_

**Server spawn doesn't handle errors; startup failures will be silent until HTTP requests fail.**

Lines 165-167 spawn the server task but don't check if `axum::serve` returns an error. If the server fails to bind or crashes during startup, the subsequent `http_get` calls at lines 176 and 184 will fail with connection errors rather than reporting the root cause. Consider adding a channel or shared flag to confirm server readiness, or at least log spawn errors.





<details>
<summary>♻️ Optional improvement to log server errors</summary>

```diff
-    tokio::spawn(async move {
-        let _ = axum::serve(listener, app).await;
-    });
+    tokio::spawn(async move {
+        if let Err(e) = axum::serve(listener, app).await {
+            eprintln!("Server error: {e}");
+        }
+    });
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/main.rs` around lines 165 - 169, The
spawned server task currently ignores axum::serve errors and the main test
continues after a fixed sleep; change the tokio::spawn block to capture
axum::serve()'s Result and propagate startup status to the test: create a
oneshot (or watch) channel before spawning, pass the sender into the spawned
task, attempt to bind/run axum::serve(listener, app).await inside the task, send
Ok(()) on successful start (or send an error/Err or the error string if binding
fails), and log any error from axum::serve; in the main task, replace the fixed
tokio::time::sleep with awaiting the receiver with a timeout, fail the test or
print the serve error if the receiver returns an error, and only then proceed to
the http_get calls so startup failures are reported instead of silent connection
errors (refer to tokio::spawn, axum::serve, and http_get in your edits).
```

</details>

<!-- cr-comment:v1:9d37c1dc446e0787844e6d75 -->

In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/record_source.rs`:
- Around line 126-170: The code currently defaults required fields to empty
strings (variables did, collection, rkey) which leads to invalid AT-URIs and
primary-key collisions; change the extraction logic so that if did, collection,
or rkey are missing or empty after parsing you return a ParseOutcome::Malformed
(same pattern used for missing commit/unknown operation) instead of using
unwrap_or("") — update the did assignment (variable did), the collection
extraction (variable collection) and the rkey extraction (variable rkey) to
validate presence/non-empty and return a Malformed error with raw_text when they
are absent/empty; this will prevent at_uri() from producing invalid URIs and
stop malformed events from overwriting records.

> 
> **Percent-encode `wantedCollections` / `wantedDids` (and `cursor`) when building the Jetstream subscribe URL**  
> `JetstreamSource::url()` concatenates raw values into the WebSocket query string without percent-encoding; if any value contains reserved query characters (e.g., `&` or `=`), the resulting URL can be mis-parsed and server-side filtering can fail. Encode the values (or use `url::Url`/a query builder) and add the dependency (`experiments/appview-validation/Cargo.toml` currently has no `urlencoding`/`url` crate).
> 
> <details>
> <summary>Proposed fix</summary>
> 
> ```diff
>      pub fn url(&self) -> String {
>          let mut url = format!("wss://{}/subscribe", self.host);
>          let mut first = true;
>          for c in &self.wanted_collections {
>              url.push(if first { '?' } else { '&' });
>              url.push_str("wantedCollections=");
> -            url.push_str(c);
> +            url.push_str(&urlencoding::encode(c));
>              first = false;
>          }
>          for d in &self.wanted_dids {
>              url.push(if first { '?' } else { '&' });
>              url.push_str("wantedDids=");
> -            url.push_str(d);
> +            url.push_str(&urlencoding::encode(d));
>              first = false;
>          }
>          if let Some(c) = self.cursor {
>              url.push(if first { '?' } else { '&' });
>              url.push_str("cursor=");
> -            url.push_str(&c.to_string());
> +            url.push_str(&urlencoding::encode(&c.to_string()));
>          }
>          url
>      }
> ```
> </details>
> 
> <details>
> <summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>
> 
> ```
> Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
> rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.
> 
> In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/jetstream.rs` around lines 62 - 84,
> JetstreamSource::url currently concatenates raw wanted_collections, wanted_dids,
> and cursor into the query string which can break when values contain reserved
> characters; fix by percent-encoding each query value before appending (encode c
> and d strings and c.to_string() for cursor) — e.g., add and use a crate like
> url::form_urlencoded or percent-encoding to encode values when building the
> query, update experiments/appview-validation/Cargo.toml to add the chosen
> dependency, and replace the manual
> push_str(c)/push_str(d)/push_str(&c.to_string()) calls in JetstreamSource::url
> with encoded-value appends (keep the same parameter names "wantedCollections",
> "wantedDids", "cursor").
> ```
> 
> </details>
> 
> <!-- cr-comment:v1:c9149a0bc3161d192db9bb65 -->
> 
> </blockquote></details>
> 
> </blockquote></details>

<details>
<summary>🧹 Nitpick comments (2)</summary><blockquote>

<details>
<summary>experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/publish.rs (2)</summary><blockquote>

`220-231`: _💤 Low value_

**`RefCell` usage is safe here but unconventional; consider a simpler pattern.**

Since the closure is synchronous (`FnMut`, not async), `RefCell` works, but a mutable local captured by the closure is cleaner:

```rust
let mut found = false;
src.run(|outcome| {
    if let ParseOutcome::Commit { event, .. } = outcome {
        let _ = index.apply(&event, stats);
        if event.rkey == rkey {
            found = true;
            return false;
        }
    }
    true
})
.await?;
Ok(found)
```

This avoids the `RefCell` import and `.borrow_mut()` ceremony.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/publish.rs` around lines 220 - 231,
Replace the RefCell-based flag with a simple mutable local bool: change "let
found = RefCell::new(false);" to "let mut found = false;", inside the src.run
closure assign "found = true;" instead of "*found.borrow_mut() = true;", keep
the early-stop "return false" behavior, and after ".await?;" return or use
"found" (e.g., "Ok(found)"). This touches the src.run call and the
ParseOutcome::Commit branch where index.apply(&event, stats) and rkey are
referenced; also remove the RefCell import since it will no longer be needed.
```

</details>

<!-- cr-comment:v1:ab28fa0e3224e49e652088b1 -->

---

`198-200`: _💤 Low value_

**`rkey_of` silently returns empty string on malformed URIs.**

If `at_uri` is empty or ends with `/`, this returns `""` which would cause a downstream `deleteRecord` to fail or target the wrong record. Consider returning `Result` or panicking early to surface malformed responses.

```diff
-fn rkey_of(at_uri: &str) -> String {
-    at_uri.rsplit('/').next().unwrap_or("").to_string()
+fn rkey_of(at_uri: &str) -> Result<String> {
+    at_uri
+        .rsplit('/')
+        .next()
+        .filter(|s| !s.is_empty())
+        .map(str::to_string)
+        .ok_or_else(|| anyhow!("malformed AT URI, no rkey: {at_uri}"))
 }
```

Then update call sites to use `rkey_of(&post_uri)?`.

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/publish.rs` around lines 198 - 200,
The rkey_of function currently returns an empty String for malformed URIs which
can silently cause wrong deletes; change its signature from fn rkey_of(at_uri:
&str) -> String to return a Result<String, E> (e.g., Result<String,
anyhow::Error> or Result<String, String>) and validate that at_uri is non-empty
and does not end with '/' (use at_uri.rsplit('/').next() and check it's
non-empty), returning Err(...) on failure; then update all call sites that
currently call rkey_of(&post_uri) to use the fallible form (e.g.,
rkey_of(&post_uri)?), propagating the Result or handling the error accordingly
so malformed URIs surface immediately instead of producing empty rkeys.
```

</details>

<!-- cr-comment:v1:e1049f39a3c8e42629c16107 -->

### Review — coderabbitai — COMMENTED — 2026-06-13T12:57:20Z

**Actionable comments posted: 3**

> [!CAUTION]
> Some comments are outside the diff and can’t be posted inline due to platform limitations.

> 
> 
> <details>
> <summary>⚠️ Outside diff range comments (1)</summary><blockquote>
> 
> <details>
> <summary>experiments/appview-validation/src/index.rs (1)</summary><blockquote>
> 
> `88-92`: _⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_
> 
> **`apply_batch` ignores `Delete` actions and upserts everything.**
> 
> This breaks event semantics: delete events can leave rows present (or recreate sparse rows), so indexed state diverges from source stream for `Action::Delete`.
> 
>    
> 
> <details>
> <summary>Suggested fix</summary>
> 
> ```diff
>  pub fn apply_batch(&mut self, events: &[RecordEvent]) -> Result<usize> {
>      let tx = self.conn.transaction()?;
>      for ev in events {
> -        upsert_row(&tx, ev)?;
> +        match ev.action {
> +            Action::Delete => {
> +                delete_row(&tx, &ev.at_uri())?;
> +            }
> +            Action::Create | Action::Update => {
> +                upsert_row(&tx, ev)?;
> +            }
> +        }
>      }
>      tx.commit()?;
>      Ok(events.len())
>  }
> ```
> </details>
> 
> <details>
> <summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>
> 
> ```
> Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
> rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.
> 
> In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/index.rs` around lines 88 - 92,
> apply_batch currently upserts every RecordEvent regardless of its Action, which
> causes Delete events to be ignored; modify apply_batch to inspect each ev.action
> (use the RecordEvent.action or Action enum) and for Action::Delete call the
> delete handling function (e.g., delete_row(&tx, ev)) or perform the appropriate
> DELETE SQL instead of upsert_row, while preserving the existing upsert for
> Action::Upsert/Insert/Update; ensure you still use the same transaction (tx) and
> propagate errors from delete_row/upsert_row, then commit and return the correct
> processed count.
> ```
> 
> </details>
> 
> <!-- cr-comment:v1:47e802950a56b0b185904a38 -->
> 
> </blockquote></details>
> 
> </blockquote></details>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the

Inline comments:
In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/atproto.rs`:
- Around line 88-93: The shared reqwest client built by client() has no timeouts
and can hang; update reqwest::Client::builder() in client() to set a
connect_timeout (e.g., Duration::from_secs(5)) and an overall timeout (e.g.,
Duration::from_secs(30)) using the connect_timeout(...) and timeout(...) builder
methods, importing std::time::Duration as needed so the client applies
reasonable connect and read/write timeouts for calls to base, pds, and
PLC_DIRECTORY.

In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/bootstrap.rs`:
- Line 145: The calls that currently ignore results (e.g., "let _ =
index.apply(&event, &mut stats);" and the similar "index.save_cursor" usages)
must check and handle the Result instead of discarding errors: replace the
ignored-result pattern with explicit Result handling (match or the ? operator)
so failures are logged and propagated (or cause the bootstrap to return an Err)
rather than being suppressed; apply the same change to every occurrence of
index.apply and index.save_cursor in the stream callbacks so write/checkpoint
errors fail the bootstrap instead of being ignored.

In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/index.rs`:
- Around line 173-181: load_cursor currently swallows all rusqlite errors by
calling query_row(...).ok(), which converts I/O/schema/corruption failures into
Ok(None); change load_cursor to call
self.conn.query_row(...).map(Some).or_else(|e| match e {
rusqlite::Error::QueryReturnedNoRows => Ok(None), other => Err(other.into()), })
so that only "no row" becomes Ok(None) and all other rusqlite errors are
propagated as Err; keep the same bound for name (?1) and return the same
Result<Option<i64>> type so callers like checkpoint_at_crash/bootstrap will see
real DB errors instead of being treated as a missing checkpoint.

---

Outside diff comments:
In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/index.rs`:
- Around line 88-92: apply_batch currently upserts every RecordEvent regardless
of its Action, which causes Delete events to be ignored; modify apply_batch to
inspect each ev.action (use the RecordEvent.action or Action enum) and for
Action::Delete call the delete handling function (e.g., delete_row(&tx, ev)) or
perform the appropriate DELETE SQL instead of upsert_row, while preserving the
existing upsert for Action::Upsert/Insert/Update; ensure you still use the same
transaction (tx) and propagate errors from delete_row/upsert_row, then commit
and return the correct processed count.
```

<details>

`118-127`: _⚡ Quick win_

**Silent CBOR parse errors hide validation data.**

Lines 122 and 126 skip malformed frames without logging. For a validation experiment reporting "hypothesis vs reality," parse failures are valuable findings that reveal protocol edge cases or implementation issues.





<details>
<summary>📊 Proposed change to log parse failures</summary>

```diff
         let mut rdr = IoCursor::new(&bytes[..]);
         let header: Cbor = match ciborium::from_reader(&mut rdr) {
             Ok(v) => v,
-            Err(_) => continue,
+            Err(e) => {
+                eprintln!("  [frame {frames}] CBOR header parse error: {e}");
+                continue;
+            }
         };
         let body: Cbor = match ciborium::from_reader(&mut rdr) {
             Ok(v) => v,
-            Err(_) => continue,
+            Err(e) => {
+                eprintln!("  [frame {frames}] CBOR body parse error: {e}");
+                continue;
+            }
         };
```

```

In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/labeler.rs` around lines 118 - 127,
The code silently skips CBOR parse failures when reading header and body (the
matches that call ciborium::from_reader into header: Cbor and body: Cbor);
change those Err(_) arms to capture the error (e) and log it with context (e.g.,
which stage—header or body—failed, the error e, and any frame/index or a short
hex/snippet of bytes/rdr position) instead of just continuing, so malformed
frames are recorded for later analysis; keep the existing continue after logging

Inline comments:
In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/firehose.rs`:
- Around line 145-154: The current code uses elapsed (from start) captured
before awaiting consumer, which inflates busy_frac; change to capture
producer_elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64() before drop(tx)/consumer.await
and use producer_elapsed to compute ev_rate/frame_rate/mb_s (keeping the
producer-window rates), then after awaiting the consumer (consumer.await)
recompute total_elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64() and set busy_frac =
busy.as_secs_f64() / total_elapsed * 100.0 so busy_frac reflects the full
runtime; update references to elapsed accordingly (use producer_elapsed for
rates and total_elapsed for busy_frac).
- Around line 85-94: The consumer currently ignores errors from
Index::apply_batch and increments the consumed counter regardless; change the
task to propagate failures by returning a Result from the consumer and only
increment consumed after apply_batch succeeds. Concretely: check the Result from
idx.apply_batch(&buf) instead of discarding it, on Err return or propagate the
error from the consumer task (do not advance consumed or clear buf), and on Ok
increment consumed and clear buf and update busy (keeping the Instant::now
timing around the successful call). Ensure the consumer's signature and caller
are updated to accept/propagate the Result so failures aren't turned into false
“kept up” successes.

In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/labeler.rs`:
- Around line 72-75: The WebSocket URL construction using endpoint.replace only
handles "https://" and fails for "http://"; update the wss construction (the wss
variable where format!("...{}", endpoint.replace(...))) to handle both schemes
by parsing endpoint with url::Url::parse and switching its scheme to "wss" for
"https" and "ws" for "http" (or, if you prefer a simpler fix, perform two
replacements: first replace "https://" -> "wss://", then "http://" -> "ws://");
then use the updated URL when building the subscribe URL so both http and https
endpoints produce valid ws/wss URLs.

Nitpick comments:
In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/labeler.rs`:
- Around line 118-127: The code silently skips CBOR parse failures when reading
header and body (the matches that call ciborium::from_reader into header: Cbor
and body: Cbor); change those Err(_) arms to capture the error (e) and log it
with context (e.g., which stage—header or body—failed, the error e, and any
frame/index or a short hex/snippet of bytes/rdr position) instead of just
continuing, so malformed frames are recorded for later analysis; keep the
existing continue after logging if you still want to skip malformed frames.
```

### coderabbitai[bot] on `experiments/appview-validation/src/record_source.rs` :L171 — 2026-06-13T12:15:29Z

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Empty-string defaults corrupt the index by creating duplicate AT-URIs.**

Lines 127, 164, and 169 default `did`, `collection`, and `rkey` to empty strings when missing or invalid. Line 72's `at_uri()` then produces invalid URIs like `"at:////"` for multiple malformed events. Since `index.rs` uses the AT-URI as the PRIMARY KEY (line 33), subsequent malformed events with missing fields will silently overwrite earlier ones, conflating distinct records.

Return `ParseOutcome::Malformed` instead when any of these required fields are missing or empty after extraction.





<details>
<summary>🛡️ Proposed fix to validate required fields</summary>

```diff
     let cursor = raw.get("time_us").and_then(Value::as_i64).unwrap_or(-1);
-    let did = raw.get("did").and_then(Value::as_str).unwrap_or("").to_string();
+    let did = match raw.get("did").and_then(Value::as_str) {
+        Some(s) if !s.is_empty() => s.to_string(),
+        _ => {
+            return ParseOutcome::Malformed {
+                error: "missing or empty `did`".into(),
+                raw_text: text.chars().take(500).collect(),
+            };
+        }
+    };

     // Prediction P2: I expect `cid` at the top level next to `did`. The parser
     // *first looks where I predicted*, then falls back to where it really is, so
     // the report can tell whether my prediction held.
     let top_level_cid = raw.get("cid").and_then(Value::as_str).map(str::to_string);

     let commit = match raw.get("commit") {
         Some(c) => c,
         None => {
             return ParseOutcome::Malformed {
                 error: "kind=commit but no `commit` object".into(),
                 raw_text: text.chars().take(500).collect(),
             };
         }
     };

     let action = commit
         .get("operation")
         .and_then(Value::as_str)
         .and_then(Action::parse);
     let action = match action {
         Some(a) => a,
         None => {
             return ParseOutcome::Malformed {
                 error: format!(
                     "unknown/absent operation: {:?}",
                     commit.get("operation")
                 ),
                 raw_text: text.chars().take(500).collect(),
             };
         }
     };

-    let collection = commit
-        .get("collection")
-        .and_then(Value::as_str)
-        .unwrap_or("")
-        .to_string();
-    let rkey = commit
-        .get("rkey")
-        .and_then(Value::as_str)
-        .unwrap_or("")
-        .to_string();
+    let collection = match commit.get("collection").and_then(Value::as_str) {
+        Some(s) if !s.is_empty() => s.to_string(),
+        _ => {
+            return ParseOutcome::Malformed {
+                error: "missing or empty `commit.collection`".into(),
+                raw_text: text.chars().take(500).collect(),
+            };
+        }
+    };
+    let rkey = match commit.get("rkey").and_then(Value::as_str) {
+        Some(s) if !s.is_empty() => s.to_string(),
+        _ => {
+            return ParseOutcome::Malformed {
+                error: "missing or empty `commit.rkey`".into(),
+                raw_text: text.chars().take(500).collect(),
+            };
+        }
+    };
     let record = commit.get("record").cloned();
```
</details>

### coderabbitai[bot] on `experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/local.rs` :L73 — 2026-06-13T12:40:22Z

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Error suppression could hide indexing failures.**

Line 68 silently drops `index.apply` errors with `let _ =`. While this is test code, suppressing errors makes it impossible to distinguish between "all fixtures indexed successfully" and "some fixtures failed to index." If apply fails (e.g., SQL constraint violation, schema mismatch), the test continues without signaling the problem.

Consider propagating the error or at least logging failures.





<details>
<summary>🔍 Proposed fix to surface errors</summary>

```diff
     src.run(|outcome| {
         if let ParseOutcome::Commit { event, .. } = outcome {
-            let _ = index.apply(&event, stats);
+            if let Err(e) = index.apply(&event, stats) {
+                eprintln!("Warning: failed to index fixture {}: {e}", event.at_uri());
+            }
         }
         true
     })
```

</details>

<!-- suggestion_start -->

<details>
<summary>📝 Committable suggestion</summary>

> ‼️ **IMPORTANT**

            }
        }
        true
    })
    .await
}

### coderabbitai[bot] on `experiments/appview-validation/src/atproto.rs` :L93 — 2026-06-13T12:57:19Z

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_

**Add timeouts to shared reqwest client (`client()`)**

`client()` builds a `reqwest::Client` without `connect_timeout` or `timeout`, so calls to `base`, `pds`, and `PLC_DIRECTORY` can hang indefinitely on stalled DNS/TLS/socket. Add reasonable connect/read timeouts.

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
 pub fn client() -> reqwest::Client {
     reqwest::Client::builder()
         .user_agent("appview-validation-experiment/0.1")
+        .connect_timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(5))
+        .timeout(std::time::Duration::from_secs(20))
         .build()
         .expect("build reqwest client")
 }

### coderabbitai[bot] on `experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/bootstrap.rs` :L145 — 2026-06-13T12:57:19Z

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Do not suppress index/checkpoint errors in the stream callbacks.**

Line 145, Line 193, Line 194, Line 223, and Line 224 ignore `index.apply` / `index.save_cursor` failures. That can make the bootstrap proofs report success even when writes/checkpoints failed.

   

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix pattern (apply to both stream phases)</summary>

```diff
+let stream_err: RefCell<Option<anyhow::Error>> = RefCell::new(None);
 src.run(|outcome| {
     if let ParseOutcome::Commit { event, .. } = outcome {
-        let _ = index.apply(&event, &mut stats);
-        let _ = index.save_cursor("crash-demo", event.cursor);
+        if let Err(e) = index.apply(&event, &mut stats) {
+            *stream_err.borrow_mut() = Some(e);
+            return false;
+        }
+        if let Err(e) = index.save_cursor("crash-demo", event.cursor) {
+            *stream_err.borrow_mut() = Some(e);
+            return false;
+        }
         // existing stop condition...
     }
     true
 })
 .await?;
+if let Some(e) = stream_err.into_inner() {
+    return Err(e);
+}
```
</details>


Also applies to: 193-194, 223-224

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/bootstrap.rs` at line 145, The calls
that currently ignore results (e.g., "let _ = index.apply(&event, &mut stats);"
and the similar "index.save_cursor" usages) must check and handle the Result
instead of discarding errors: replace the ignored-result pattern with explicit
Result handling (match or the ? operator) so failures are logged and propagated
(or cause the bootstrap to return an Err) rather than being suppressed; apply
the same change to every occurrence of index.apply and index.save_cursor in the
stream callbacks so write/checkpoint errors fail the bootstrap instead of being
ignored.
```

</details>

awk 'NR>=150 && NR<=260 {printf "%5d  %s\n", NR, $0}' "$FILE"

echo
echo "---- index.rs cursor_state table + open() ----"
FILE2="experiments/appview-validation/src/index.rs"
awk 'NR>=1 && NR<=140 {printf "%5d  %s\n", NR, $0}' "$FILE2"
```

Repository: croftc/SecurityPolicy

</details>

**`load_cursor` masks SQLite failures as “no checkpoint”**  
`load_cursor` uses `query_row(...).ok()`, so *any* rusqlite error (I/O/corruption/schema) becomes `Ok(None)`. In `experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/bootstrap.rs`, that makes the restart path treat the persisted cursor as missing (leading to `anyhow!("no checkpoint")`), and the earlier `checkpoint_at_crash` reporting can silently show `0`. The `name` parameter is safely bound via `?1`, but recovery behavior/diagnostics are at risk.  

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
-use rusqlite::Connection;
+use rusqlite::{Connection, OptionalExtension};
...
 pub fn load_cursor(&self, name: &str) -> Result<Option<i64>> {
-    Ok(self
-        .conn
-        .query_row(
-            "SELECT time_us FROM cursor_state WHERE name = ?1",
-            [name],
-            |r| r.get(0),
-        )
-        .ok())
+    Ok(self
+        .conn
+        .query_row(
+            "SELECT time_us FROM cursor_state WHERE name = ?1",
+            [name],
+            |r| r.get(0),
+        )
+        .optional()?)
 }
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/index.rs` around lines 173 - 181,
load_cursor currently swallows all rusqlite errors by calling
query_row(...).ok(), which converts I/O/schema/corruption failures into
Ok(None); change load_cursor to call
self.conn.query_row(...).map(Some).or_else(|e| match e {
rusqlite::Error::QueryReturnedNoRows => Ok(None), other => Err(other.into()), })
so that only "no row" becomes Ok(None) and all other rusqlite errors are
propagated as Err; keep the same bound for name (?1) and return the same
Result<Option<i64>> type so callers like checkpoint_at_crash/bootstrap will see
real DB errors instead of being treated as a missing checkpoint.
```

</details>

### coderabbitai[bot] on `experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/firehose.rs` :L94 — 2026-06-13T13:13:44Z

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**Stop counting failed batches as indexed.**

Line 86 and Line 92 discard `Index::apply_batch` errors, then Line 88 and Line 93 still advance `consumed`. Any SQLite failure here turns into a false “kept up” result while dropping that batch on the floor. Return a `Result` from the consumer task and only increment `consumed` after a successful write.  
  

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
-    let consumer = tokio::spawn(async move {
+    let consumer = tokio::spawn(async move -> Result<(usize, Duration)> {
         let mut idx = Index::open("firehose.sqlite").expect("open index");
         let mut buf: Vec<RecordEvent> = Vec::with_capacity(BATCH);
         let mut consumed = 0usize;
         let mut busy = Duration::ZERO;
         while let Some(ev) = rx.recv().await {
@@
             }
             let t = Instant::now();
-            let _ = idx.apply_batch(&buf);
+            idx.apply_batch(&buf)?;
             busy += t.elapsed();
             consumed += buf.len();
             buf.clear();
         }
         if !buf.is_empty() {
-            let _ = idx.apply_batch(&buf);
+            idx.apply_batch(&buf)?;
             consumed += buf.len();
         }
-        (consumed, busy)
+        Ok((consumed, busy))
     });
@@
-    let (consumed, busy) = consumer.await?;
+    let (consumed, busy) = consumer.await??;
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/firehose.rs` around lines 85 - 94, The
consumer currently ignores errors from Index::apply_batch and increments the
consumed counter regardless; change the task to propagate failures by returning
a Result from the consumer and only increment consumed after apply_batch
succeeds. Concretely: check the Result from idx.apply_batch(&buf) instead of
discarding it, on Err return or propagate the error from the consumer task (do
not advance consumed or clear buf), and on Ok increment consumed and clear buf
and update busy (keeping the Instant::now timing around the successful call).
Ensure the consumer's signature and caller are updated to accept/propagate the
Result so failures aren't turned into false “kept up” successes.
```

</details>

### coderabbitai[bot] on `experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/firehose.rs` :L154 — 2026-06-13T13:13:44Z

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟡 Minor_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**`busy_frac` is not using actual wall time.**

`busy` includes post-window drain work, but `elapsed` is captured before Line 148 waits for the consumer to finish. If the queue still has backlog after the 15s ingest window, the printed “write-busy % of wall time” is inflated and can exceed 100%. Keep `ev_rate` on the producer window, but compute `busy_frac` from total runtime through `consumer.await`.  
  

<details>
<summary>Suggested fix</summary>

```diff
-    let elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
+    let producer_elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();
     drop(tx); // close queue -> consumer drains and finishes
     let _ = ws.close(None).await;
-    let (consumed, busy) = consumer.await?;
+    let (consumed, busy) = consumer.await??;
+    let total_elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64();

     // ---- results ----
-    let ev_rate = produced as f64 / elapsed;
-    let frame_rate = frames as f64 / elapsed;
-    let mb_s = bytes as f64 / elapsed / 1_000_000.0;
-    let busy_frac = busy.as_secs_f64() / elapsed * 100.0;
+    let ev_rate = produced as f64 / producer_elapsed;
+    let frame_rate = frames as f64 / producer_elapsed;
+    let mb_s = bytes as f64 / producer_elapsed / 1_000_000.0;
+    let busy_frac = busy.as_secs_f64() / total_elapsed * 100.0;
```
</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/firehose.rs` around lines 145 - 154,
The current code uses elapsed (from start) captured before awaiting consumer,
which inflates busy_frac; change to capture producer_elapsed =
start.elapsed().as_secs_f64() before drop(tx)/consumer.await and use
producer_elapsed to compute ev_rate/frame_rate/mb_s (keeping the producer-window
rates), then after awaiting the consumer (consumer.await) recompute
total_elapsed = start.elapsed().as_secs_f64() and set busy_frac =
busy.as_secs_f64() / total_elapsed * 100.0 so busy_frac reflects the full
runtime; update references to elapsed accordingly (use producer_elapsed for
rates and total_elapsed for busy_frac).
```

</details>

### coderabbitai[bot] on `experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/labeler.rs` :L75 — 2026-06-13T13:13:44Z

_⚠️ Potential issue_ | _🟠 Major_ | _⚡ Quick win_

**URL scheme replacement only handles `https://`, not `http://`.**

The `replace("https://", "wss://")` at line 74 fails when `endpoint` starts with `http://` (not `https://`), leaving the wrong scheme and producing an invalid WebSocket URL like `http://example.com/xrpc/...`.

While the hardcoded Bluesky DID uses `https://`, a validation experiment should handle both schemes correctly.





<details>
<summary>🔧 Proposed fix using proper scheme replacement</summary>

```diff
-    let wss = format!(
-        "{}/xrpc/com.atproto.label.subscribeLabels?cursor=0",
-        endpoint.replace("https://", "wss://")
-    );
+    let ws_scheme = if endpoint.starts_with("https://") {
+        endpoint.replace("https://", "wss://")
+    } else if endpoint.starts_with("http://") {
+        endpoint.replace("http://", "ws://")
+    } else {
+        endpoint.clone()
+    };
+    let wss = format!(
+        "{}/xrpc/com.atproto.label.subscribeLabels?cursor=0",
+        ws_scheme
+    );
```

</details>

<details>
<summary>🤖 Prompt for AI Agents</summary>

```
Verify each finding against current code. Fix only still-valid issues, skip the
rest with a brief reason, keep changes minimal, and validate.

In `@experiments/appview-validation/src/bin/labeler.rs` around lines 72 - 75, The
WebSocket URL construction using endpoint.replace only handles "https://" and
fails for "http://"; update the wss construction (the wss variable where
format!("...{}", endpoint.replace(...))) to handle both schemes by parsing
endpoint with url::Url::parse and switching its scheme to "wss" for "https" and
"ws" for "http" (or, if you prefer a simpler fix, perform two replacements:
first replace "https://" -> "wss://", then "http://" -> "ws://"); then use the
updated URL when building the subscribe URL so both http and https endpoints
produce valid ws/wss URLs.
```

</details>
